serving.config_yaml: # extra_llm_api_options — qwen3-coder-30b (bf16 MoE) on DGX Spark (playbook standard shape). # free_gpu_memory_fraction is a kv_cache_config key, enable_padding a cuda_graph_config key # (TRT-LLM 1.2.1 rejects them as top-level LLM args). disable_overlap_scheduler: true kv_cache_config: free_gpu_memory_fraction: 0.8 # CUDA graphs DISABLED for the VANILLA MoE path: the unfused reference MoE # (fused_moe_vanilla.py:547) performs a host-syncing op (dynamic expert routing) that is # illegal inside a CUDA-graph capture region -> "operation not permitted when stream is # capturing" -> cudaErrorStreamCaptureInvalidated at executor init. Running eager avoids it. cuda_graph_config: null # MoE backend pin for qwen bf16 MoE on GB10 (sm_121a consumer Blackwell), TRT-LLM 1.3.0rc9. # Both fused paths are broken on this SM target: # - AUTO/CUTLASS -> selects the SM80 (Ampere) fused_moe kernel -> Blackwell shared-memory # assert ("GPU lacks the shared memory resources to run fused_moe kernel", # fused_moe_gemm_launcher_sm80.inl:79); every autotuner tactic fails -> executor deadlock # (server ready, GPU idle, requests never reach the executor). # - TRITON -> matmul_ogs kernel emits PTX using '.tile::gather4 with .shared::cluster', a # datacenter-Blackwell/Hopper feature NOT supported on sm_121a -> ptxas fatal at autotuner # warmup -> "Executor worker returned error" at startup. # VANILLA is the unfused reference PyTorch MoE (no custom kernels) -> compiles on any SM. Slow, # but acceptable for the runtime bridge cells (we only need correctness + runtime metrics, not # peak throughput). nemotron works on CUTLASS because its MoE is NVFP4 (Blackwell FP4 path), a # different kernel from the bf16 fused path that breaks here. moe_config: backend: VANILLA

sanity_check.prompt: Write a Python function `nth_prime(n)` that returns the n-th prime number (1-indexed), with a short docstring and three example assertions. Then explain in two sentences how it works.

fairness_note: Runtime is TensorRT-LLM (vs vLLM for gpt-oss/qwen baselines). Quality metrics are model-driven and comparable across runtimes; throughput/energy/latency are runtime-dependent and must be read with the qwen/gpt-oss dual-runtime bridge. seed, temperature, and the OpenCode version are held identical to the vLLM runs.
